name: running-tests
description: Runs tests and handles failures. Triggered when: test execution, verification, test failures, CI checks.
capabilities: Detects test commands by checking project files like package.json, Cargo.toml, justfile, Makefile, pyproject.toml, and go.mod
Runs test suites and reports pass or fail status with test counts
Identifies failed test names and extracts error messages
Analyzes failure causes to determine if the issue is an implementation bug, test bug, or environment problem
Implements fixes for implementation bugs and test bugs
Re-runs tests after fixes to confirm all tests pass
author: 1gy
securitySummary: All 20 static findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged markdown formatting (backticks), metadata hashes (SHA-256 checksum), and YAML frontmatter as security threats. This is a legitimate test runner skill that uses the Bash tool to execute test commands. No network calls, no credential access, no persistence mechanisms.
valueStatement: Manually running tests and analyzing failures is time-consuming. This skill automates test execution across different project types, detects the correct test command, runs tests, and handles failures by implementing fixes.
limitations: Does not modify complex multi-file test configurations
Cannot resolve external dependencies that tests require
Limited to projects with standard test command patterns

useCases.title: Run Project Test Suites
useCases.description: Execute project tests automatically with the correct command for your project type without manual setup

useCases.title: Validate CI Test Execution
useCases.description: Test your code in CI environments and diagnose failures quickly to keep pipelines running

useCases.title: Verify Test Coverage
useCases.description: Run tests to verify code changes pass all quality checks before approving merges

promptTemplates.title: Run All Tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the tests for this project and tell me how many tests passed or failed

promptTemplates.title: Debug Failures
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the tests, identify any failures, and explain what is causing them

promptTemplates.title: Fix and Verify
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the tests, fix any failures, and confirm all tests pass afterward

promptTemplates.title: Prepare for CI
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the full test suite and fix any issues so the code is ready for CI checks

bestPractices: Run tests frequently during development to catch issues early before they compound
Use specific test commands when you need targeted testing of particular files
Review failure analysis to understand root causes before applying automatic fixes
antiPatterns: Running tests without first checking if the project has a test configuration
Ignoring test failures and committing broken code to the repository
Applying automatic fixes without understanding the root cause of the failure

faq.question: What test frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Supports npm test, cargo test, pytest, go test, make test, and just test commands. Auto-detects based on project files.

faq.question: Can it run specific test files?
faq.answer: The skill runs the default test command. You can ask for specific test filtering in your prompt for targeted testing.

faq.question: Does it work with CI systems?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill works with any project that has standard test commands configured for CI environments.

faq.question: What data does this skill access?
faq.answer: Only project files needed to detect test commands. No sensitive data, credentials, or private information is accessed.

faq.question: What if tests fail due to environment issues?
faq.answer: The skill distinguishes between bugs and environment issues. Environment problems are reported to you for manual resolution.

faq.question: How does it compare to manual test running?
faq.answer: This skill automates finding the right test command, running tests, and diagnosing failures. It saves time on setup and analysis.
